heading: Time-limited appointments
label: 1608 Time-limited appointments
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1608 was renumbered .section 1623 of this title
Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1996 Amendment note under .Oct. 1, 1996section 1635 of Pub. L. 104–201section 1593 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection effective , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1996 Amendment note under .Oct. 1, 1996section 1635 of Pub. L. 104–201section 1593 of this title

children.heading: .—Authority for Time-Limited Appointments
children.label: a .—Authority for Time-Limited Appointments
children.text: The Secretary of Defense may by regulation authorize appointing officials to make time-limited appointments to defense intelligence positions specified in the regulations.

children.heading: .—Review of Use of Authority
children.label: b .—Review of Use of Authority
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall review each time-limited appointment in a defense intelligence position at the end of the first year of the period of the appointment and determine whether the appointment should be continued for the remainder of the period. The continuation of a time-limited appointment after the first year shall be subject to the approval of the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Condition on Permanent Appointment to Defense Intelligence Senior Executive Service
children.label: c .—Condition on Permanent Appointment to Defense Intelligence Senior Executive Service
children.text: An employee serving in a defense intelligence position pursuant to a time-limited appointment is not eligible for a permanent appointment to a Defense Intelligence Senior Executive Service position (including a position in which the employee is serving) unless the employee is selected for the permanent appointment on a competitive basis.

children.heading: .—Time-Limited Appointment Defined
children.label: d .—Time-Limited Appointment Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “time-limited appointment” means an appointment (subject to the condition in subsection (b)) for a period not to exceed two years.
